# Transcrição: 5_Steps_To_Deliver_A_Heartfelt_Apology

- **Fonte:** 5_Steps_To_Deliver_A_Heartfelt_Apology
- **Duração:** 02:22
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:22]

We are all wrong at some point in life. Like me being wrong about filming outdoors in the outback, I now have dirt everywhere, especially in my drink. I also have ants in my pants, and I'm pretty sure Peter and I are going to get bitten by a snake. But look, on the topic, we are all wrong at some point in life. And what that means is, at some point in our lives, we are going to have to learn how to apologize. And if we get good at apologizing, what that means is, even when we're wrong in life, we still can use that opportunity to deepen our relationships with the people we care about. Let me share with you how I used to make apologies. And this is when my apology game was crappy. So normally what I would say is, number one, well, I'm sorry if I offended you. Number two, I would say something like, well, I'm sorry you feel that way. And then number three, I'd say something a little different because I would add an excuse to it. And I'd say, well, I'm sorry, okay, because you don't even know that I'm going through a lot right now. Geez. But ever since I realized that getting good at apologies isn't a form of weakness, but it's actually a skill that if we learn well, even when things are bad, we can deepen our relationships. So let me share with you how I do it now. Here are five steps on how you can deliver a heartfelt apology. Step one, say I'm sorry. That's right. That's a full sentence. Don't add anything else to that, especially excuses. Number two, say what you are sorry for in detail. And then step three, show that you understand why what you did was wrong. Number four, share why you are not going to allow this to happen again. And step five, if appropriate, share reparations. Let me give you an example. Hey, Dan, I'm sorry. I'm sorry that I've been making you work late multiple days in the last couple of weeks. I know that when I make you work late, it causes you not to be able to spend as much time with your family. And I know that's really, really important to you. And in order to make sure that this doesn't happen again in the future, I'm going to make sure we spread out your workload so that you never have to stay back late. And you know what? For all the days that you've worked overtime, I'm going to make sure I compensate you for that financially as well. As compared to what I would have done before, the previous version of Vin would have said, Dan, get your shit together, b****.
